title: Deep Sky Object Atlas
subtitle: Browse 4,800+ deep sky objects — filter Messier, NGC and IC catalogs by type, constellation, magnitude and best season.

score.heading: How is the Accessibility Score calculated?
score.intro: The <0>Accessibility Score</0> (out of 100) estimates how easy a deep-sky object is to photograph and to get a satisfying result from. It does not measure the object's beauty, but its accessibility for the astrophotographer. It is based on four physical properties:
score.size: <0>Apparent size:</0> larger objects are easier to frame and can be shot at short focal length, with a telephoto lens, without a high-end mount.
score.brightness: <0>Brightness (magnitude and light density):</0> a bright, concentrated object needs less exposure time than a faint, diffuse one.
score.type: <0>Object type:</0> galaxies, clusters and reflection nebulae image well in colour (RGB); emission-line targets are weighted accordingly.
score.rgb: <0>RGB / narrowband accessibility:</0> emission nebulae, planetary nebulae and supernova remnants reach their full potential in narrowband; in plain RGB they render a little less, which is taken into account.
score.universal: The score is universal: it does not depend on your location. To see what you can actually photograph tonight from your location, use the "Visible Tonight" filter.
score.note: Note: a community image-quality rating will complement this score as the Cosmic Frame community grows.

loadingCandidates: Loading candidate objects for visibility computation…
noAstroNight: No full astronomical night tonight — try <0>Nautical</0> or <1>Civil</1>.

about.summary: About the Deep Sky Object Catalog
about.p1: This catalog contains over 4,800 deep sky objects spanning the complete <0>Messier catalog</0> (110 objects), the <1>NGC</1> and <2>IC</2> catalogs, plus the <3>Sharpless</3> catalog of H-II regions. You can filter by object type — galaxies, emission nebulae, planetary nebulae, open clusters, globular clusters, dark nebulae and more — or narrow results by constellation, magnitude, angular size and best observation season.
about.p2: Every object includes an exposure guide with recommended fast and deep integration times, a photogenicity score that ranks how rewarding a target is to photograph, and a framing preview that shows how the object fits in your telescope and camera field of view. Use these tools to plan your next deep sky imaging session with confidence.
